avoid: Do not promote this candidate despite the intended good strategy; correctness failure dominates.
Do not spend time optimizing tile size, vector width, occupancy, or launch overhead until the failed shapes pass.
Do not hardcode d=4096 behavior or hidden dimensions.
Do not replace the dominant W @ X with a custom full GEMM; that is high risk and unlikely to beat cuBLAS.
Do not accept small relative error if the harness fails on max absolute error.
evidence: Correctness failed for 2 of 3 required shapes: d=3584 and d=4608.
Failures are finite and have very small relative L2 error, but max absolute error reaches 0.000366 for d=3584 and 0.00293 for d=4608, exceeding the harness tolerance.
d=4096 passes, so the implementation is not universally broken, but lack of shape dispatch plus shape-dependent failures makes it unsafe.
Benchmark data is unavailable because correctness failed; performance cannot be considered.
Static review and compilation passed, so the issue is likely numerical/order/indexing behavior in the fused rank-16 update rather than a build problem.

recommended_actions: check rank-16 indexing and vector alignment
Fix correctness before any performance tuning.
Compare candidate output against a decomposition: first verify Y = W @ X alone matches the baseline, then verify Z = B.T @ X, then verify only the fused Y += A @ Z kernel.
Check the fused update kernel for row/column indexing mistakes, vectorized float4 store alignment assumptions, and any out-of-bounds or cross-row writes.
Because all failing/passing dimensions are multiples of 128, do not focus only on tail-column handling; also inspect grid-stride logic, leading dimension usage, and row-major contiguous indexing.

If the error is purely from accumulation-order differences in the rank-16 update, make the custom kernel numerically closer to the reference: use deterministic FP32 accumulation over k=0..15, avoid fast math, avoid reassociation, and ensure the add is exactly Y[row, col] += sum_k A[row, k] * Z[k, col].
Add per-shape validation during development for d=3584, 4096, and 4608; do not rely on the single passing 4096 case.
After correctness is fixed, benchmark against the cuBLAS/ATen W@X and B.T@X plus one fused rank-16 update kernel target.
